node.statement: (Image of Φ lies in F_{S(A)}(φ)). For T in the domain, ω_T belongs to the face generated by φ: F_{S(A)}(φ). Proof: Since 0 ≤ T ≤ ||T||·I (as T ∈ π_φ(A)' is positive and bounded), we have ω_T ≤ ||T||·φ (in the sense that ||T||·φ - ω_T is positive). For T with ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1, this means ω_T is dominated by a scalar multiple of φ. A state ψ belongs to F_{S(A)}(φ) if and only if ψ is dominated by some scalar multiple of φ (i.e., there exists λ > 0 with ψ ≤ λφ). More precisely, ψ ∈ F_{S(A)}(φ) iff there exists λ > 0 such that λφ - ψ is a positive functional. Since ω_T ≤ ||T||φ, this condition is satisfied.
